# Conroe ISD Board Meeting — 2018-02-20

# Unofficial Meeting Notes
## Conroe Independent School District Board of Trustees
### Regular Meeting — February 20, 2018

---

*These are unofficial notes based on an auto-generated transcript and may contain errors or omissions.*

---

## Opening

- **Moment of Silence** held in remembrance of:
  - Victims of the school shooting in Parkland, Florida
  - **Mr. Kirk Slan** (phonetic), a retired CISD educator who served the district for over 40 years and passed away the prior week
- **Invocation** delivered by Board President Bush, asking for comfort for grieving families, continued protection of students and staff, and wisdom in decision-making
- **Color Guard Presentation** by JROTC cadets

---

## Recognitions

### TMEA All-State Music Students
**Presenter:** Bob Horton, Coordinator of Fine Arts

- **32 Conroe ISD students** were named Texas Music Educators Association (TMEA) All-State musicians
- The All-State selection process begins with over **68,000 students statewide** across 33 TMEA regions; fewer than **3% (approximately 1,700)** earn All-State designation
- Students represented multiple high schools: Conroe High School, Oak Ridge High School, The Woodlands High School, and The Woodlands College Park High School
- Students were recognized across **orchestra, band, and choir** categories
- Numerous fine arts teachers were acknowledged for their support
- **President Bush** offered extended congratulations, emphasizing the board's commitment to fine arts education, the scientific benefits of music study, and encouraging students to continue pursuing music

---

## Public Participation

### Speaker: Teresa de Mayo (age 16)
- Identified herself as the Texas state representative for **Be Strong Global**, an organization focused on bullying prevention
- Addressed the board regarding **school safety and bullying**, prompted by recent national events and personal experience
- Shared a personal account from **October 17, 2017**, in which a friend expressed thoughts of committing a school shooting at an CISD school due to being bullied. She recorded the conversation and reported it to her mother, the Montgomery County Sheriff's Department, and CISD police
- Stated that law enforcement spoke to the student but she felt insufficient action was taken
- Reported that she and her mother emailed school principals and counselors with videos but received **no response**; the student reportedly received only a few days of in-school suspension
- Made several requests/pleas to the board:
  - Listen more attentively to students reporting concerns
  - Arm teachers with **resources and knowledge** rather than weapons
  - Consider **metal detectors** in schools
  - Invest in bullying prevention programs
  - Do not dismiss student concerns as trivial
- Shared that an 8th-grade teacher once intervened and prevented her own suicide, emphasizing the importance of caring adults in schools
- **President Bush** thanked her for her courage and respectful presentation, but noted the board could not discuss or act on the matter as it was **not a posted agenda item**

---

## Consent Agenda (Item 3)

- No items were requested for removal
- **Motion to approve:** Made and seconded
- **Vote: Approved unanimously (voice vote)**

---

## Action Items

### Item 4A — Naming of Principal, Schneider Elementary
- **Dr. Stockton** recommended **Crystal Poncho** (phonetic) as the new principal of Schneider Elementary
- Ms. Poncho thanked the board, acknowledged mentors (Principal Lindsey Ardoin, Dr. Edith Upshaw, Dr. Christine Butler), and introduced her family
- **Vote: Approved unanimously (voice vote)**

### Item 5A — Guaranteed Maximum Price (GMP) Amendment: New K-6 School, Oak Ridge Feeder Zone (Flex 19)
**Presenter:** EZ Foster

- **Marshall Construction Company** was selected as CM-at-Risk on April 18, 2017
- Negotiated GMP: **$24,986,119**
- School to be located in the Oak Ridge feeder zone
- **Vote: Approved unanimously (voice vote)**

### Item 5B — Award of RFQ 18-01-01: Two-Way Radios for Transportation Department
**Presenter:** EZ Foster and team (Bob King, Sam Davila, Sgt. Matt Latelock, Steve Mirror)

- Recommended awarding contract to **Motorola Solutions** through the HGAC cooperative contract
- Estimated expenditure: **$975,967.88**
- Five vendors submitted responses meeting specifications; Motorola had the **best price** among qualifying vendors
- Key goals of the new radio system:
  - Enable direct communication with the Police Department during emergencies (no patching)
  - Utilize Montgomery County's tower infrastructure for improved, reliable coverage
  - Latest digital technology; expandable as district grows
  - Monthly tower use fee to county; eliminates district's need to maintain its own towers
- **Board discussion:** Questions about lowest bid — clarified that two lower bids did not meet specifications. Discussion about coverage reliability across the large district geography, especially referencing Hurricane Harvey communications challenges.
- **Vote: Approved unanimously (voice vote)**

### Item 6A — Award of RFP 17-01-018: Contracted Educational Services, Professional Development & Consulting
**Presenter:** Darrin Rice

- Recommended awarding contracts to **13 vendors** listed on attached tabulation
- Estimated annual expenditure: **$8,000** (for this batch)
- To date, **202 vendors out of approximately 350** have been awarded since June 2017
- Total included in previously awarded annual estimate of **$1.5 million**
- Contracts firm through **February 28, 2019**, with option to renew annually for four additional one-year terms (through February 2023)
- **Vote: Approved unanimously (voice vote)**

---

## Information/Reports

### Item 5C — Capital Improvement Updates
**Presenter:** EZ Foster

- **Grand Oaks High School:** Scheduled to open **August 2018**; on schedule; ~90% closed in; exterior site work (turn lanes, athletic venues, fire lanes) taking shape; interior finishes progressing (softball turf installed, practice gyms, restroom fixtures)
- **Katherine Johnson Clark Intermediate:** Scheduled to open **August 2018**; on schedule; focus on closing in front of building; interior finishes (library, hallway painting) progressing
- **Lifecycle 2018 Projects:** Work underway at North Maintenance Facility; planning for spring break work at various campuses; summer master planning in progress
- **Conroe High School Addition:** Building addition on schedule for completion **December 2018**; central plant largely complete with equipment in place; major mechanical renovation of main campus building to follow
- All projects reported as **on schedule**

### Item 6B — Financial Reports (January)
**Presenter:** Darrin Rice

- **General Fund Cash & Investments:** $345.3 million total
  - State pool investments: $234 million
  - Short-term investments: $59.4 million
  - Longer-term investments (TCG): $51.9 million
- **General Fund:** Projected increase to fund balance of approximately **$5 million**
- **Child Nutrition:** Projected increase of approximately **$970,000**
- **2015 Bond Referendum Status:**
  - Expended/encumbered: **$347.4 million**
  - Estimate to complete: **$166.4 million** (includes Flex 19 ~$28M and new junior high)
  - Total project forecast: **$513.8 million**
  - Remaining contingency: **~$6.4 million** of the **$520 million** bond program
- **Self-Funded Insurance:** January showed a monthly loss (attributed to flu season), but year-to-date performance remains positive — revenues of ~$20 million vs. expenses of ~$17 million (surplus of ~$3 million); wellness center participation strong (~$640 supplant figure)
- **Investment Portfolio:** Total par value $714.9 million; combined yield ~1.52%; benchmark 90-day T-bill at 1.43%

### Item 7A — Local Policy Manual Update 110
**Presenter:** Ms. Gloves (phonetic)

- Small update containing **one local policy** revision
- Prompted by the legislative session; reorganized the board election section (BE section) for improved usability
- Streamlined language, added information, included details about terms and elections
- Board will be asked to **adopt next month**; no action taken at this meeting

---

## Closed (Executive) Session

- Board entered closed session at **6:46 PM** under Section 551.074 of the Texas Open Meetings Act
- No final actions, decisions, or votes from closed session were reported in the transcript
